title: apply_event() as a pure state transition method keeps state logic testable and separated from render code
description: AppState::apply_event takes &mut self and an AppEvent and applies exactly one state transition with no egui dependency. This means it can be unit-tested by constructing an AppState::default(), calling apply_event with a hand-crafted event, and asserting the resulting field values — no egui Context, no frame, no window required. If state transitions were inlined in App::update(), they would be entangled with layout code, impossible to test in isolation, and harder to reason about because the render and state-mutation concerns would be interleaved. A contributor might suggest inlining apply_event into update() to reduce indirection. That would trade testability for a marginal reduction in call depth. The separation is load-bearing: tests for state correctness (e.g. MessagesLoaded is ignored if chat_id does not match selected_chat) can be written and maintained without an egui runtime.
